4e2fdb7a-de73-49a0-b2d5-6119a050e914: Future Echoes was recorded primarily in Lynch's home studio on Eigg, with additional sessions at Chem19 Studios in Hamilton, Scotland. The album features Lynch playing most instruments himself, including synthesizers, drum machines, and guitars, creating layered pop arrangements. Producer Paul Savage, known for his work with Mogwai and The Delgados, mixed the record. This was Lynch's third full-length album as Pictish Trail, following Secret Soundz and Island Family, and marked a shift toward more electronic-influenced indie pop compared to his earlier folk-leaning material.
351de38f-3624-4c97-8059-7559b9eaf504: Island Family was recorded primarily at Lynch's home studio on Eigg during the COVID-19 pandemic lockdowns. The album was produced by Lynch himself and features contributions from various musicians recorded remotely, reflecting the isolation themes of the period. The record incorporates synthesizers, drum machines, and Lynch's characteristic layered vocals alongside traditional rock instrumentation. It marks his fifth full-length release under the Pictish Trail name and explores more electronic textures than his earlier folk-influenced work.
aa0f3de1-3703-4b40-9087-4606d0270ef0: Thumb World was recorded primarily at Lynch's home studio on the Isle of Eigg during 2019 and early 2020. The album was produced by Lynch himself, incorporating field recordings from his remote island environment alongside drum machines, synthesizers, and acoustic instruments. It marked his fifth full-length release under the Pictish Trail moniker, following 2018's "Far Gone (And Out)." The sound combines intimate folk vocals with layered electronic textures, programmed beats, and ambient soundscapes that reflect both the isolation and natural beauty of his island home.
1aa41b19-5a72-341b-bd91-4cf61d1dab6b: Doolittle was recorded at Downtown Recorders in Boston and Carriage House Studios in Stamford, Connecticut, with producer Gil Norton. The album was the band's second full-length release and featured a more polished sound compared to their earlier work, incorporating cleaner production while maintaining their signature dynamic shifts between soft verses and explosive choruses. The recording sessions included extensive use of Santiago's Fender guitars through Marshall amplifiers and Deal's prominent bass lines on her Fender Precision Bass. The album showcased the band's range from the acoustic-driven "Here Comes Your Man" to the aggressive "Tame" and the Biblical allegory of "Gouge Away."
ac9b0a0c-0da6-387f-8c09-65783187346a: Trompe le Monde was recorded at Master Control Studios in Burbank, California with producer Gil Norton, who had previously worked with the band on Doolittle. The album was the band's fourth and final studio release before their initial breakup in 1993, featuring a heavier guitar sound and more polished production than their earlier work. The recording sessions included the use of multiple guitar overdubs and effects processing, with Black Francis incorporating references to space and UFOs throughout the lyrics. The album represents the band's most commercially accessible sound while maintaining their characteristic dynamic shifts between quiet verses and explosive choruses.
90bf604b-bde5-3b45-bfd0-ba775be89986: Meds is Placebo's fifth studio album, recorded at Olympic Studios in London with producer Dimitri Tikovoi. The album marked the band's continued exploration of electronic elements alongside their guitar-driven sound, incorporating synthesizers, drum machines, and digital effects processing. Guest vocalist Alison Mosshart from The Kills appears on the title track "Meds." The record features a more polished production than previous efforts while maintaining the band's characteristic blend of alternative rock with electronic textures and pop sensibilities.
5142d975-9bd8-31c4-9094-545573f679f0: Love in the Fascist Brothel was recorded in 2005 and served as the band's second full-length album following their 2003 debut Dissertation, Honey. The album was produced with a raw, unpolished approach that emphasized the band's confrontational sound and featured distorted guitars, pounding drums, and shouted vocals. The recording captured the group's live energy and maintained their reputation for combining punk aggression with art-damaged sensibilities. The album continued the band's exploration of political themes through both their lyrics and abrasive musical approach.
22b6c7cf-d1b0-43ed-9ef5-84aaa417790d: Chasing Hamburg was recorded in 2009 and serves as the band's second full-length album following their 2007 debut Sometimes Things Just Disappear. The album was produced with a focus on capturing the band's live energy while incorporating more polished studio elements than their previous work. The record features guitar-driven arrangements with prominent bass lines and dynamic drumming, showcasing a sound that balances aggressive rock passages with melodic indie sensibilities. 3fe04efd-5e7d-32a0-b96b-924c818c59b0: Exploded Drawing was recorded in 1996 and marked the band's third full-length album. The record was produced by Bob Weston and captured the band's signature sound of interlocking, dissonant guitar parts played in alternate tunings by Ash Bowie and Dave Brylawski. a0783aad-4d48-460f-9be8-2357f86d83d9: Do Whatever You Want All the Time was Ponytail's second and final studio album, recorded in Baltimore and released on We Are Free Records in 2011. The album was produced by the band themselves and features their signature sound of wordless vocals layered over intricate guitar work and rhythmic percussion. 006f3bc8-57f3-3045-85f1-51ff6962c2f6: Ice Cream Spiritual was Ponytail's second and final album, released in 2009 on We Are Free Records. The record features Siegel's wordless vocal improvisations over the band's instrumental compositions, creating layered soundscapes that blend punk rhythms with psychedelic guitar textures. b65f1e21-32fb-3a14-805f-be37d0f9d6b8: Kamehameha was Ponytail's debut album, recorded in 2006 and released on the We Are Free label. feacf7ed-8429-401a-b9eb-0040725325be: Pre's self-titled debut album was recorded in 1992 and marked the band's entry into the symphonic rock scene. 90aae241-8cb0-3672-a909-c7ac8b5c99b2: Everything She Touched Turned Ampexian was recorded in 2008 and released on Warp Records in 2009. The album was produced entirely by Herren using his signature cut-and-paste sampling technique, layering chopped vocal fragments over glitchy beats and atmospheric textures. It marked his fourth full-length album as Prefuse 73, following his trilogy of Vocal Studies releases. The record features downtempo grooves built around manipulated vocal samples, sparse drum programming, and ambient electronic elements that create a more subdued sound compared to his earlier, more frenetic work.
6b2b7de3-c00b-3a5f-9ba1-a6825e70db34: Extinguished: Outtakes was released in 2003 on Warp Records as a collection of unreleased tracks and alternate versions from sessions spanning Herren's early work. The album was compiled from material recorded between 2000-2002, featuring the same sample-chopping techniques and MPC programming that characterized his previous releases. The tracks showcase Herren's approach of layering fragmented vocal samples over irregular drum patterns and synthesizer work. This compilation sits between his breakthrough album Vocal Studies + Uprock Narratives and The Failing Lights in his discography, offering a glimpse into his creative process during those formative years.
5d03c0a2-d622-42c8-ae03-6be87a5a9e05: Forsyth Gardens was recorded in Herren's home studio in Savannah, Georgia, where he had relocated from New York. The album features collaborations with vocalists including Shara Worden of My Brightest Diamond and rapper Busdriver, alongside instrumental tracks built from chopped-up samples and live instrumentation. Herren used vintage drum machines, analog synthesizers, and field recordings captured around his neighborhood to create the album's sound palette. The record marked a return to more beat-oriented material after several years of ambient and experimental releases, positioning it as a middle-period work that bridges his earlier hip-hop productions with his later atmospheric explorations.
cbe78742-3f6f-3f68-8ec9-847b6c19f8cc: One Word Extinguisher was recorded in 2003 and released on Warp Records, marking Prefuse 73's debut for the British electronic label. The album was produced entirely by Herren using an MPC sampler and various analog equipment, incorporating chopped-up vocal samples, manipulated drum breaks, and layered synthesizer work. It features collaborations with vocalists including Mikah 9, Divine Styler, and Sam Dew across its 21 tracks. The record sits as the third full-length in Prefuse 73's discography and showcases a more refined approach to the project's characteristic stuttering rhythms and dense sample collages compared to earlier releases.
c5a8568c-6624-42c5-873c-b6e6ecabfd1e: Rivington Não Rio was recorded in New York City and Rio de Janeiro, with Herren collaborating with Brazilian musicians and incorporating field recordings from both cities. The album features live instrumentation including guitar, bass, and drums alongside Herren's characteristic chopped-up samples and programmed beats. It serves as his tenth studio album under the Prefuse 73 name and represents a more groove-oriented direction compared to his earlier glitch-heavy releases. The sound combines downtempo electronic production with Brazilian rhythms and jazz-influenced chord progressions.
199c44fc-d95b-329f-a8ed-c33ea8fbbfc6: Security Screenings was recorded in 2005 and released on Warp Records in 2006. The album was produced entirely by Herren using his signature cut-up sampling technique, layering chopped vocal snippets over glitchy drum patterns and ambient soundscapes. It marked Prefuse 73's second full-length release for Warp following 2003's One Word Extinguisher. 819a7d0f-bb17-3420-9999-d37af41ef57b: Surrounded by Silence was recorded in Herren's home studio and marked his third full-length album as Prefuse 73, following One Word Extinguisher in 2003. 6ab005db-7dba-3287-a4cb-e3df9bf70db2: The 92 vs 02 Collection was Prefuse 73's debut full-length album, released on Warp Records in 2002. The album was largely created using an MPC sampler and features heavily manipulated samples layered over programmed drum patterns. Most tracks were recorded in Herren's home studio and showcase his technique of cutting up existing recordings into fragmented, stuttering arrangements. The collection established the Prefuse 73 sound of glitchy, downtempo electronic music with hip-hop influenced rhythms and served as his introduction to the IDM scene.
a32b87c8-2ad6-31a5-b488-954aa2df9ac7: Good Health was the band's debut full-length album, recorded in 2002 and released on Lookout! Records. The album was produced by John Goodmanson at Robert Lang Studios in Seattle, featuring angular guitar work, driving bass lines, and the interplay between Fudesco and Zollo's contrasting vocal styles. cb4238e8-2d11-38a3-bd28-ff558cd8a7ee: The New Romance was the band's debut full-length album, recorded at Robert Lang Studios in Shoreline, Washington with producer John Goodmanson. The album showcased the band's combination of driving punk rhythms, angular guitar work, and intertwined male-female vocals across eleven tracks. a7c4f177-8de8-366f-87f4-aa851ae32012: Sailing the Seas of Cheese was recorded at Different Fur Studios in San Francisco with producer Matt Winegar. The album features Claypool's six-string fretless bass prominently throughout, alongside LaLonde's guitar work and Alexander's precise drumming. This was the band's second studio album and their major label debut on Interscope Records, following their independent release Frizzle Fry. The record combines heavy, syncopated rhythms with unconventional song structures and Claypool's nasal vocal delivery over topics ranging from fishing to childhood memories.
b9dd6193-4cb7-4ea0-8dd7-e92bb9109f1d: Purple Rain was recorded at various Minneapolis studios including Warehouse Studio and First Avenue nightclub between 1983 and 1984. The record served as both a studio album and soundtrack to Prince's semi-autobiographical film of the same name. The songs blend rock guitar work with synthesizers and drum machines, featuring contributions from The Revolution including Wendy Melvoin, Lisa Coleman, and Bobby Z.
dcccfd93-aa36-388c-a884-99547cb3b989: "Breathe" was recorded at Liam Howlett's home studio in Essex and produced by Howlett himself. The track features Keith Flint's distinctive growled vocals over a foundation of chopped-up breakbeats, distorted synthesizers, and samples from The Incredible Bongo Band's "Apache." The song appeared on The Prodigy's third album "The Fat of the Land" and became one of their most recognizable tracks. The production emphasizes heavy compression and filtering effects on the drum breaks, creating the characteristic punchy sound that defined the big beat movement of the mid-1990s.
f9fa083f-db00-3048-99e7-5b186672bb09: Music for the Jilted Generation was recorded at Earthworks Studios in London and produced by Liam Howlett. The album was created in response to the UK government's Criminal Justice Act, which restricted rave culture and outdoor gatherings. Howlett expanded the band's sound beyond their debut's hardcore rave template, incorporating breakbeat rhythms, distorted guitar samples, and aggressive vocal samples from Keith Flint and Maxim Reality. The record sits as the band's second studio album, bridging their early underground rave work with the more rock-influenced approach they would take on later releases.
c0dda61e-08cd-34c9-a502-30dbb30fa207: The Only Thing I Ever Wanted was recorded in the duo's home studio in London and released in 2006 as their second full-length album. The album features Psapp's characteristic use of children's toys, kitchen utensils, and homemade instruments alongside electronic programming and Durant's whispered vocals. Producer and additional musician credits include work by the band members themselves, with guest appearances from various session musicians on strings and additional percussion. eaf860cb-52e1-3abd-985f-e7ed847d4c8c: Album was recorded in New York and Los Angeles with producer Bill Laswell, who brought a more commercial rock approach to the band's sound. This was PIL's fifth studio album and marked a deliberate shift toward accessible alternative rock after their more experimental earlier releases. 25350a5f-2d80-36a4-ad87-55301f2e68b0: The Flowers of Romance was recorded in 1981 at Manor Studios in Oxfordshire with the band producing alongside Nick Launay. e3593b14-57a9-4e0e-9425-72a135048a4f: This is PiL marks the band's ninth studio album and their first release in twenty years, recorded primarily at Pressure Drop Studios in New York. e8cc1e58-d49a-3c27-9358-18205a69a88b: This is What You Want... This Is What You Get was recorded in 1983-1984 and produced by the band members themselves. edcb81d2-327c-40f0-8c65-1d4cd06bc2be: What the World Needs Now... was recorded at Eastcote Studios in London and produced by Youth (Martin Glover). 22e8c78f-29ce-42f5-b884-7d0357447153: This self-titled debut album was recorded at Electrical Audio in Chicago with producer Steve Albini in 2009. 1a5b2c66-8164-4159-8e85-274e27d222f6: Reality Cheques was recorded in 2022 and marked the band's return after a seven-year hiatus following their 2015 album The Haze. f3f18294-f40a-3720-9353-c479431a2a08: R&B Transmogrification was recorded in 1997 and released on Up Records, marking the band's third full-length album. c92f73ee-527f-42ed-a556-fd615941e214: Like Clockwork was recorded at Pink Duck Studios in Burbank, California with producer Josh Homme handling most duties alongside additional production from Mark Rankin.
